name: Michel Petrucciani
abstract: Michel Petrucciani (French pronunciation: [miʃɛl petʁutʃani]; Italian: [petrutˈtʃaːni]; 28 December 1962 – 6 January 1999) was a French jazz pianist. From birth he had osteogenesis imperfecta, a genetic disease that causes brittle bones and, in his case, short stature.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Sarah Vaughan and Joe Williams
Stanley Jordan, Bobby McFerrin, and Michel Petrucciani

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: Michel and I played some concerts in Sicily... Michel and I played a concert in, in Norway one time... we listened to one another and reacted
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: Actually I even remember a gig the two of you were playing in the Village one night that Michel Petrucciani came and was heckling you. ... He was a mean little guy sometimes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
